title: Nephrodium gracilescens
taxonomy: Nephrodium gracilescens belongs to the family Polypodiaceae, which is a part of the larger group of ferns known as Polypodiales. This species is a type of fern, classified under the division Pteridophyta. Within the class Polypodiopsida, it is specifically classified as an order Polypodiales. At the family level, it is categorized under Polypodiaceae, a family of ferns commonly known as polypods. It is further classified under the genus Nephrodium, which is now considered to be a synonym for the genus Polypodium.

Here's a paragraph on Nephrolepis gracilis:

Nephrolepis gracilis, also known as the delicate chain fern, is a species of fern in the family Nephrolepidaceae. The delicate chain fern has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made from Nephrolepis gracilis include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
